reason: The fixture_edits leave the good fixture dirty after shipping, so this fp-1.00 agent does not cleanly recover. good/meaningful-assert/change.patch adds TWO tests, and the baseline flagged BOTH: (1) test_resolved_log_level_prod_is_info (verified verbatim duplicate of the template's parametrized test_resolved_log_level_is_info_outside_dev over ['staging','prod','ci'] at template/tests/unit/test_settings.py.jinja:18-20), and (2) test_slo_request_latency_p99_env_override, which reviewers flagged as redundant low-severity in r0 and r2 because the template's test_env_vars_override (line 27-32) already proves a float field (APP_SLO_ERROR_RATE_PCT) is env-overridden and pydantic-settings coerces every scalar identically. The change's replace-redundant-test edit ONLY removes the log-level duplicate; it never touches the SLO env-override test. After the fix lands, the good fixture STILL draws a redundant-coverage finding on the SLO test. Combined with the prompt rewrite that re-anchors redundant/duplicate coverage to low/info, that finding no longer blocks at block_threshold=high, so the fixture trivially 'passes' the gate WITHOUT being clean -- it will not yield a literal []. The note claims the coupled fix drives fp from 1.00 down, but it cannot reach 0 on the primary good fixture because a real redundant-coverage over-flag remains by construction; the precision win is masked by the threshold semantics rather than earned by a genuinely-clean fixture. Recommend tightening the fixture_edit to also collapse test_slo_request_latency_p99_env_override into the existing env-override idiom before shipping. DEFAULT-refute uncertainty on the unaddressed second over-flag stands.
